impact: The root layout currently runs startup secret validation, so a missing required runtime secret can block public/static/legal pages as well as secret-dependent workflows. This matches the current startup-fail acceptance, but future route handlers or runtime clients may need route-scoped validation or an accessible maintenance/error boundary to keep unaffected public pages available.
resolution: Split startup validation into a public-safe root layout check and a stricter secret-required runtime check. The root layout now validates public env safety, optional Cognito public identifiers, and stage syntax without requiring backend secret identifiers, while secret-dependent routes such as Stripe webhooks still load and validate required secrets through the runtime startup source before processing.
title: Root startup secret gate can block public pages
